records.ety: [L. obsequiae, pl., funeral rites, fr. obsequi: cf. F. obsques. See Obsequent, and cf. Obsequious.]

defs: The last duty or service to a person, rendered after his death; hence, a rite or ceremony pertaining to burial; -- now used only in the plural.
quotes: I will . . . fetch him hence, and solemnly attend, With silent obsequy and funeral train.
I will myself Be the chief mourner at his obsequies.
The funeral obsequies were decently and privately performed by his family.
